channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-11625] ls -l returns "ls: cannot access Invalid argument"
item.description:
After upgrading from 2.7 to 2.10.5 unable to access 97050 files all in the same directory
Here is an example

bash-4.3$ lfs getstripe -d .
stripe_count:  -1 stripe_size:   1048576 stripe_offset: -1

bash-4.3$ lfs path2fid .
[0x224179d4c:0x1a0a:0x0]

bash-4.3$ ls -l Eta.0000707880.data
ls: cannot access 'Eta.0000707880.data': Invalid argument

bash-4.3$ lfs path2fid Eta.0000707880.data
[0x224179d4c:0x2883:0x0]

Attaching lfs getstripe out  and stat of each object.

Eta.0000707880.data.obj_stats
item.summary: ls -l returns "ls: cannot access Invalid argument"

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
Oleg Drokin (green@whamcloud.com) merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/34304/
Subject: LU-11625 ofd: handle upgraded filter_fid properly
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_12
Current Patch Set:
Commit: e18486abdb1192bbb7402a5970521e6d52ced382

Minh Diep (mdiep@whamcloud.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/34304
Subject: LU-11625 ofd: handle upgraded filter_fid properly
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_12
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: e19de1850fcc80791a7a701f9290534104aed0e9

Oleg Drokin (green@whamcloud.com) merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/33958/
Subject: LU-11625 ofd: handle upgraded filter_fid properly
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_10
Current Patch Set:
Commit: e23385f9b0184462e42c2a00fc46d43650d6b1cc
mhanafi no, you don't need to change the clients. the idea is that the server upgrades LOV EA properly.
Is the patch required on the client side? Or can this fix work work older clients?
Landed for 2.13

Oleg Drokin (green@whamcloud.com) merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/33627/
Subject: LU-11625 ofd: handle upgraded filter_fid properly
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 381a8cdd527ce4deccfc3f7eb461892f6f2f3fff

Mahmoud

The patch is undergoing pre-landing testing ATM so hopefully will land in the coming days.

Peter
Please provide an update on the status of the patch.

Andreas Dilger (adilger@whamcloud.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/33958
Subject: LU-11625 ofd: handle upgraded filter_fid properly
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_10
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 6d77bd656efcf3c5e2f9e934f5c6dd55d69fecba
Has there been any additional progress on this patch.
mhanafi please don't use this patch yet - it's still under testing.

Alex Zhuravlev (bzzz@whamcloud.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/33906
Subject: LU-11625 ofd: handle upgraded filter_fid properly
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_10
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 46e0e2cb167df8a7644883ac95ec2da77fce6199

I did try abort recovery but it didn't work.

Unless we take a cluster wide dedicated time it will be difficult to cleanly umount all clients. So I think the patch will be a better options for us. How much rework does the patch require to be consider safe.

/** multiple RPCs in flight //* multiple RPCs in flight */

#define OBD_INCOMPAT_MULTI_RPCS 0x00000400

iirc, this flag is reset when all clients are unmounted. is it possible to start newer MDS, unmount the client (or abort recovery to get all the clients evicted) ?

Alex

Could you please assist with this?

Thanks

Peter

Downgrade to 2.7.3 couldn't mount MDT

00000001:00020000:15.0:1544742465.414368:0:6712:0:(tgt_lastrcvd.c:1072:tgt_server_data_init()) nbp8-MDT0000: unsupported incompat filesystem feature(s) 400

I need to try again when I am able to umount all clients. I think a patch would be better. What about a lfsck patch to update the objects.

What about running with the patch for a short time (maintenance window). Going back to 2.7.x would require downgrading the OS to cent6.x but if we can run with the patch for a short time it may be a short maintenance.

Mahmoud, sorry for the slow reply on this, we've migrated mail servers and I missed your previous comment.

As for downgrading, I would not necessarily recommend going back to 2.9 if you upgraded from 2.7.x. You might consider to downgrade to 2.7.x for a short time in a maintenance window to copy the files and then return to 2.10.x again. If any files have been created with 2.10 features (e.g. PFL composite layouts) then those files will not be accessible under 2.7.x, so it should be OK to downgrade for a short time but I wouldn't recommend it for an extended uptime given that you have been running 2.10.x for some time already. Also, given that there are still some latent problems in the filesystem the fewer files that are accessed/modified while running 2.7.x the better.
If the patch going to take some time, do we need to go back to 2.7.x or can we go back to 2.9 copy the files.
Any upates?

I was going to port this to b2_10 but I see that the master version of this patch has failed sanity-scrub with problems that look related to the patch, so I don't think it is safe to put into production. I was en route to SC today, but I'll try to look at it later tonight.
Yes I think so.  Yesterday Andreas said it is low risk. We run just to copy the files and then revert.

Mahmoud

Again, are you wanting this ahead of the patch being finalized?

Peter
Can we get a 2.10.5 back port please.

Andreas Dilger (adilger@whamcloud.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/33627
Subject: LU-11625 ofd: handle upgraded filter_fid properly
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: aea6a732fd01bc204c3d0a888faf09b52e757e5a

Attaching s219.dk.out.gz The client is 10.151.26.25@o2ib.

s219.dk.out.gz

Looks like osd_check_lma() returning -22

00080000:00000001:5.0:1541523220.922495:0:10991:0:(osd_handler.c:517:osd_iget_check()) Process entered
00080000:00000001:5.0:1541523220.922495:0:10991:0:(osd_handler.c:646:osd_iget_check()) Process leaving via put (rc=0 : 0 : 0x0)
00080000:00000001:5.0:1541523220.922496:0:10991:0:(osd_handler.c:728:osd_check_lma()) Process entered
00080000:00000001:5.0:1541523220.922500:0:10991:0:(osd_handler.c:748:osd_check_lma()) Process leaving (rc=18446744073709551594 : -22 : ffffffffffffffea)
00080000:00000001:5.0:1541523220.922501:0:10991:0:(osd_handler.c:1201:osd_fid_lookup()) Process leaving via out (rc=18446744073709551594 : -22 : 0xffffffffffffffea)

I'd guess that this is a very old filesystem? Do you know with which Lustre version it was first formatted? The files in question all date from Nov 2013, which puts it days after 2.5 released, or more likely some 2.4.x release. The MDT inode FID sequence numbers are very large (not that this is a problem in itself, the sequence numbers are 64-bit values and you've used only 600M of them, but just a confirmation of my guess).

The "trusted.fid" xattr on the OSTs that stores a backpointer with the FID of the MDT inode that is using the object has grown over time, as file layouts became more complex (e.g. stripe_size, stripe_count, component_<start,end>, component_id for PFL layouts). The trusted.fid xattrs for the OST objects look to be valid from the debugfs stat output, and are correctly referencing the parent FID and storing the stripe count.

The OI Scrub code should upgrade old pre-2.5 trusted.fid to store the parent FID into the trusted.lma xattr, so that it would be consistent between MDTs and OSTs, but it seems like OI Scrub was never run on some of the OSTs since 2.5 was released? I see two different forms of fid xattr on the objects:

------ service213 115 -----
Inode: 19432703   Type: regular    Mode:  0666   Flags: 0x80000
Generation: 3229129909    Version: 0x00000000:01a4c126
User:  9440   Group: 26209   Size: 1048576
File ACL: 0
Links: 1   Blockcount: 2048
Fragment:  Address: 0    Number: 0    Size: 0
 ctime: 0x5bb7c237:00000000 -- Fri Oct  5 12:57:43 2018
 atime: 0x527b011c:00000000 -- Wed Nov  6 18:55:24 2013
 mtime: 0x527b0183:00000000 -- Wed Nov  6 18:57:07 2013
crtime: 0x527b011c:3ec25968 -- Wed Nov  6 18:55:24 2013
Size of extra inode fields: 28
Extended attributes:
  trusted.fid (32) = 4c 9d 17 24 02 00 00 00 83 28 00 00 38 00 00 00 a1 5d 22 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 
  fid: objid=2252193 seq=0 parent=[0x224179d4c:0x2883:0x0] stripe=56
EXTENTS:
(0-255):5782965760-5782966015
------ service205 185 -----
Inode: 1183292   Type: regular    Mode:  0666   Flags: 0x80000
Generation: 990691313    Version: 0x00000000:01aaf3cf
User:  9440   Group: 26209   Size: 883968
File ACL: 0
Links: 1   Blockcount: 1728
Fragment:  Address: 0    Number: 0    Size: 0
 ctime: 0x5bb7c237:00000000 -- Fri Oct  5 12:57:43 2018
 atime: 0x527b0110:00000000 -- Wed Nov  6 18:55:12 2013
 mtime: 0x527b0183:00000000 -- Wed Nov  6 18:57:07 2013
crtime: 0x527b0110:d09557f4 -- Wed Nov  6 18:55:12 2013
Size of extra inode fields: 28
Extended attributes:
  trusted.fid (32) = 4c 9d 17 24 02 00 00 00 83 28 00 00 39 00 00 00 f4 33 22 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 
  fid: objid=2241524 seq=0 parent=[0x224179d4c:0x2883:0x0] stripe=57
EXTENTS:
(0-215):340487973-340488188
------ service212 166 -----
Inode: 12104635   Type: regular    Mode:  0666   Flags: 0x80000
Generation: 1266395486    Version: 0x00000000:00000000
User:  9440   Group: 26209   Size: 0
File ACL: 0
Links: 1   Blockcount: 0
Fragment:  Address: 0    Number: 0    Size: 0
 ctime: 0x5bb7c237:00000000 -- Fri Oct  5 12:57:43 2018
 atime: 0x527b00fc:642516b8 -- Wed Nov  6 18:54:52 2013
 mtime: 0x527b00fc:642516b8 -- Wed Nov  6 18:54:52 2013
crtime: 0x527b00fc:642516b8 -- Wed Nov  6 18:54:52 2013
Size of extra inode fields: 28
Extended attributes:
  trusted.fid (16) = 4c 9d 17 24 02 00 00 00 83 28 00 00 3a 00 00 00 
  fid: parent=[0x224179d4c:0x2883:0x0] stripe=58
------ service216 66 -----
Inode: 7209791   Type: regular    Mode:  0666   Flags: 0x80000
Generation: 3980417589    Version: 0x00000000:00000000
User:  9440   Group: 26209   Size: 0
File ACL: 0
Links: 1   Blockcount: 0
Fragment:  Address: 0    Number: 0    Size: 0
 ctime: 0x5bb7c237:00000000 -- Fri Oct  5 12:57:43 2018
 atime: 0x527b00b1:c4503b20 -- Wed Nov  6 18:53:37 2013
 mtime: 0x527b00b1:c4503b20 -- Wed Nov  6 18:53:37 2013
crtime: 0x527b00b1:c4503b20 -- Wed Nov  6 18:53:37 2013
Size of extra inode fields: 28
Extended attributes:
  trusted.fid (16) = 4c 9d 17 24 02 00 00 00 83 28 00 00 9f 00 00 00 
  fid: parent=[0x224179d4c:0x2883:0x0] stripe=159

As you can see, there are trusted.fid xattrs with 16-byte size, and some with 32-bite size (the old format that also stored the objid and seq values of the object itself.

Looking at this particular file, however, it seems like this old file is striped over 160 OSTs and has a file size of 60652800 bytes, so it is not using all of the OST object, but seems otherwise intact. The client starts accessing the file but hits an error:

:
:
00020000:00000002:10.0:1541487278.411671:0:96313:0:(lov_offset.c:251:lov_stripe_intersects()) [0->-1] -> [(-1) 0->-1 (0)]
00020000:00000002:10.0:1541487278.411690:0:96313:0:(lov_offset.c:251:lov_stripe_intersects()) [0->-1] -> [(-1) 0->-1 (0)]
00000100:02020000:18.0:1541487278.412587:0:7535:0:(client.c:1266:ptlrpc_check_status()) 11-0: nbp8-OST00a6-osc-ffff881f0ecc2800: operation ldlm_enqueue to node 10.151.27.72@o2ib failed: rc = -22
00000100:02020000:6.0:1541487278.412613:0:7537:0:(client.c:1266:ptlrpc_check_status()) 11-0: nbp8-OST0054-osc-ffff881f0ecc2800: operation ldlm_enqueue to node 10.151.27.68@o2ib failed: rc = -22
00000100:02020000:26.0:1541487278.412627:0:7536:0:(client.c:1266:ptlrpc_check_status()) 11-0: nbp8-OST00ab-osc-ffff881f0ecc2800: operation ldlm_enqueue to node 10.151.27.77@o2ib failed: rc = -22
:
:
00000100:02020000:2.0:1541487278.415811:0:7524:0:(client.c:1266:ptlrpc_check_status()) 11-0: nbp8-OST0042-osc-ffff881f0ecc2800: operation ldlm_enqueue to node 10.151.27.76@o2ib failed: rc = -22

for OST indices 166, 84, 171, ..., 66. According to the file layout these are valid stripes in the file:

             :               :              :                :
	   115	       2252193	     0x225da1	             0
	   185	       2241524	     0x2233f4	             0
	   166	       2231839	     0x220e1f	             0
	   171	       2230066	     0x220732	             0
	    84	       2233835	     0x2215eb	             0
             :               :              :                :
	    66	       2234372	     0x221804	             0

that follow immediately after the last used objects in the file objid=2252193 and objid=2241524 as seen in the debugfs OST object stat above.

What it looks like is that the OST objects which were actually storing data for the file had their "trusted.fid" xattrs updated properly to store their "self" FID, but objects beyond the end of the file do not store their "self" FID unless they are actually modified by a client. Can you please capture Lustre kernel debug logs from an OSS with lctl set_param debug=-1 set while accessing some of these files. I'd like to see where the -22 = -EINVAL error is coming from, so that we can understand what needs to be fixed.

As for solving this, one option would be to downgrade the MDS and OSS temporarily to 2.7.x and rsync the files in this directory to new files, so that they will have new-format xattrs, and then delete the old files. The other option is to temporarily quarantine these files until we can patch the OST so that the objects are accessible, and separately to make an OI Scrub/LFSCK patch to repair the objects. The latter should be done at some point anyway, so that the OST objects can store their "self" FID in the LMA in case there is ever filesystem corruption in the future that dumps the objects into lost+found and they need to be recovered.
